title: Medium without CRF for Neocallimastigomycota
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2c4a","text":"This is the protocol for the standard, nutrient-rich, undefined cultivation medium without clarified rumen fluid (CRF) used within the HiPoAF project. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":83,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5b5bh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"54h9r","text":"In previous publications this medium or slight variations of it have also been referred to as \"defined medium\" (since it does not contain CRF) - this, however, is not completely true, since it does contain undefined components (yeast extract, and tryptone). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cntv0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85c3v","text":"If you need a completely defined medium, check out our protocol \"Minimal Medium for AF\".  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9b6c3","text":"Before you start, make sure you have prepared all necessary stock solutions, and have all chemicals needed at hand (see Materials section for details). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5h24i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d2t8l","text":"You will need approx.   in total to prepare the medium (depending on the volume you prepare, it can take longer or might be faster). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":22,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2okt3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b8mph","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"a1ppi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"duration","mutability":"","data":{"duration":10800,"guid":"95DFA840829A11ECB445A952F44B4781","id":0,"label":"","pause":null,"preset":[],"start":null}},"1":{"type":"safety","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"8flt","text":"Please also ensure, that all necessary safety measures are in place (see also: Safety Warnings). ","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}

materials_text:
{"blocks":[{"key":"b4tsg","text":"Here we list the ingredients of our standard, nutrient-rich AF cultivation medium (without clarified rumen fluid), and the preparation of stock solutions needed for this medium: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":46},{"style":"bold","offset":54,"length":123},{"style":"italic","offset":83,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7b8vb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8prut","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"3td80","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"16tg3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3uenh","text":"Hemin solution is prepared by dissolving   hemin powder in a 1:1 mixture of 96% ethanol and   NaOH. The solution is then filter sterilzed (0.22µm pore size) and stored at   until use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":41,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":92,"length":1},{"key":3,"offset":171,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2870o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"421ep","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"639ur","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ago65","text":"Resazurin solution is prepared by dissolving   resazurin powder in MilliQ water and filter sterilizing it (0.22µm pore size). The solution is then stored at   until use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"offset":45,"length":1},{"key":5,"offset":157,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"7v7oq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2336e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a698h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e2i9m","text":"Salt Solution I consists of   K2HPO4 dissolved in MilliQ water. It is autoclaved ( ,  ), and stored at   until use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16},{"style":"sub","offset":31,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":35,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":6,"offset":28,"length":1},{"key":7,"offset":82,"length":1},{"key":8,"offset":85,"length":1},{"key":9,"offset":103,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"1hirj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ap1m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dqobu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1evpl","text":"Salt Solution II consists per litre of   KH2PO4,    (NH4)2SO4,   NaCl,  MgSO4.7H2O, and   CaCl2.2H2O.  The ingredients are dissolved in MilliQ water one after the other. The solution is then autoclaved ( ,  ), and stored at   until use. antibiotic stock solution is prepared by slowly dissolving   Streptomycin sulfate,   Penicillin G sodium salt, and   Ampicillin sodium salt in distilled water. The mixture is then gassed with pure CO2 for  , then closed with a rubber stopper and crimped to obtain and maintain anoxic conditions. With the help of needle and syringe the solution is filter sterilized into a sterile, anoxic, closed & crimped serum bottle and stored at   until use. For standard AF cultivation, we usually use glass serum bottles with an ND20 head and a total of volume of 120 mL. In those serum bottles we do not add more than 50 mL medium, to ensure an adequate amount of head space. Alternatively, smaller serum bottles with a total volume of 60 mL also work (filled with no more than 30 mL of medium). We recommend using the bulky serum bottles rather than the long, thin ones, because the longer, thinner ones tend to easily break and are less convenient when handling the bottles. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bspqs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"o5qr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"atgq9","text":"Rubber stoppers. The bottles are usually closed with 3-legged butyl rubber stoppers or with full-body black rubber stoppers, and crimped with a metal cap. steps: Add Salt Solution I, Salt Solution II, Yeast Extract, Tryptone, Hemin Solution, Resazurin Solution, Xylan powder (as an additional C-source for AF; can be omitted), and MilliQ water to a cooking pot.

Heat the mixture and simmer it until the colour changes to dark yellow. For us with 2 L of medium in a 5 L cooking pot, this takes about 00:20:00 to 00:30:00 . Take care not to cook it too long. Otherwise you will lose a lot of water, making the medium more concentrated. You can also carefully heat the mixture in a microwave.
Carefully pour the mixture from the pot into the Erlmeyer flask. Cool it with ice-water while agitating it by magnetic stirrer and bubble it with pure CO 2 .
While the mixture is simmering, flush a large enough Erlmeyer flask (best chose one with two times the volume of the medium) with pure CO 2 for 00:02:00 .

Once the mixture has cooled to around 39 °C, add the trace elements solution, NaHCO 3 , soluble C-source (like cellobiose, glucose, etc.; if necessary) and lastly the L-cysteine-HCl. Keep gassing with CO 2 , but adjust the gas flow in order to avoid bubbling/overflowing of the medium. Bubble with CO 2 until the medium turns yellow again.

If you want to have a simple, nutrient-rich cultivation medium for anaerobic fungi without clarified rumen fluid add milled wheat straw or pressed wheat straw pellets to your serum bottles. The combination of a complex C-source like wheat straw and a less complex one like xylan helps keeping the metabolism of AF active. Using less complex C-sources like glucose or cellobiose also works well, but it might inhibit parts of the carbohydrate metabolism of AF and can give rise to bacterial or yeast contamination if you are not handling your cultures carefully. For example: for our standard culture collection medium, we use serum bottles with a total volume of 120 mL. We add 0.35 g wheat straw to those bottles, and add 45 mL medium (in step 6). We later add 0.5 mL vitamin solution only shortly before use and sub-culture 5 mL of an actively growing culture into this medium, which brings the total volume of liquids in this bottle up to 50.5 mL. Pro-tip: For culture care (not for experiments), the pressed pure wheat straw pellets (e.g. horse feed as you might find in a pet store) are way more convenient to handle! You can simply eyeball the amount needed.
Once the medium turned yellowish, set the pH of the medium to pH 6.9 with the help of NaHCO 3 powder or 5M NaOH solution.
While setting the pH, flush your serum bottles with pure CO 2 for 1-2 minutes, to minimize oxygen contamination when aliquotting your medium in step 6.

Aliquot the medium into your serum bottles under continuous flow of CO 2 in the serum bottles and the Erlmeyer flask filled with medium. Depending on the amount of medium prepared this step takes around 00:30:00 . Dispension of the medium into 120mL glass serum bottles (max. 50mL per bottle). Picture copyright: Julia Vinzelj & Nico Peer

Take the gas hose out of the serum bottle and immediately close it with a rubber stopper, then crimp the bottle with an aluminium cap. Closing of the serum bottles with 3-legged butyl-rubber stoppers after the medium has been dispensed. Picture copyright: Julia Vinzelj & Nico Peer.
Autoclave the serum bottles at 121 °C for 00:20:00 .
Store the bottles without regard to temperature for up to 2 months. 4 °C works as well as 37 °C for the duration of 2 months at least. Discard any bottles with medium that turned red.

Before use, they should be warmed to 39 °C . Also, 0.01mL vitamin solution must be added per mL of medium shortly before use by injection. If needed, antibiotics can be injected as well. Injection of Vitamin Solution into sterile, pre-warmed serum bottles shortly before use. Picture copyright: Julia Vinzelj & Nico Peer
creator: Julia Vinzelj
